segments.text: The First Calendar.

translations.zh-Hans: 最早的日历。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What is the importance of the dots, lines, and symbols engraved on stone, bones, and ivory?

translations.zh-Hans: 刻在石头、骨头和象牙上的点、线和符号有什么重要意义？

segments.text: Future historians will be in a unique position when they come to record the history of our own times.

translations.zh-Hans: 未来的历史学家记录我们这个时代的历史时，将处于一种独特的有利地位。

segments.text: They are the great mass of evidence that steadily accumulates.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们将拥有不断积累的大量证据。

segments.text: What is more, they will not have to rely solely on the written word.

translations.zh-Hans: 而且，他们不必只依靠文字记录。

segments.text: Films, videos, and CD-ROMs are just some of the bewildering amount of information they will have.

translations.zh-Hans: 电影、录像和光盘只是他们所能获得的海量信息中的一小部分。

segments.text: They will be able, as it were, to see and hear us in action.

translations.zh-Hans: 可以说，他们能够看到并听到我们活动时的情景。

segments.text: But the historian attempting to reconstruct the distant past is always faced with a difficult task.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，试图重现遥远过去的历史学家总是面临艰巨的任务。

segments.text: He has to deduce what he can from the few scanty clues available.

translations.zh-Hans: 他必须从少得可怜的现有线索中尽力推断。

segments.text: Even seemingly insignificant remains can shed interesting light on the history of early man.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使看似微不足道的遗迹，也能为早期人类史提供有趣的启示。

segments.text: Up to now, historians have assumed that calendars came into being with the advent of agriculture, for then man was faced with a real need to understand something about the seasons.

translations.zh-Hans: 迄今为止，历史学家一直认为日历随着农业的出现而产生，因为那时人类才真正需要了解季节。

segments.text: Recent scientific evidence seems to indicate that this assumption is incorrect.

translations.zh-Hans: 近期科学证据似乎表明，这一假设并不正确。

segments.text: Historians have long been puzzled by dots, lines, and symbols which have been engraved on walls, bones, and the ivory tusks of mammoths.

translations.zh-Hans: 长期以来，历史学家一直困惑于刻在石壁、骨头和猛犸象牙上的点、线和符号。

segments.text: The nomads who made these markings lived by hunting and fishing during the last Ice Age, which began about 35,000 BC and ended about 10,000 BC.

translations.zh-Hans: 留下这些标记的游牧民族在最后一个冰川时期靠狩猎和捕鱼为生；这一时期约始于公元前35000年，终于公元前10000年。

segments.text: By correlating markings made in various parts of the historians have been able to read this difficult code.

translations.zh-Hans: 通过把各地留下的标记相互对照，历史学家已经能够解读这种复杂的符号。

segments.text: They have found that it is connected with the passage of days and the phases of the moon.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们发现，它同日子的推移和月相有关。

segments.text: It is, in fact, a primitive type of calendar.

translations.zh-Hans: 实际上，这是一种原始日历。

segments.text: It has long been known that the hunting scenes depicted on walls were not simply a form of artistic expression.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们早已知道，石壁上描绘的狩猎场面并不只是一种艺术表现形式。

segments.text: They had a definite meaning, for they were as near as early man could get to writing.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们具有明确含义，因为这已是早期人类最接近文字的表达方式。

segments.text: It is possible that there is a definite relation between these paintings and the markings that sometimes accompany them.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些图画与有时伴随其出现的标记之间，可能存在明确联系。

segments.text: It seems that man was making a real effort to understand the seasons 20,000 years earlier than has been supposed.

translations.zh-Hans: 看来，人类为了解季节所作的真正努力，比原先认为的时间早了两万年。
